title: Reduce memory pressure in US daily price bundle sync
description: US daily_refresh Price Refresh failed when app.tasks.cache_tasks.smart_refresh_cache worker was killed by SIGKILL during GitHub daily price bundle sync. Evidence from local Docker logs: task 8560a97e-7409-4fbc-829d-8419549584e4 received at 2026-06-29T20:30:01Z, price_refresh.load_universe finished at 20:30:04Z, price_refresh.sync_github_seed started at 20:30:04Z, then ForkPoolWorker-1 exited with signal 9 at 20:30:34Z. docker inspect stockscreenclaude-celery-datafetch-1 reported OOMKilled=true. Postgres logged unexpected EOF on client connection with open transaction at the same second. US active universe is about 10,059 symbols with roughly 4.36M recent price rows, versus HK about 2,806 symbols and HK completed. Likely root cause is DailyPriceBundleService.import_daily_price_bundle loading the entire .json.gz payload via json.load and building pandas DataFrames for all symbols before chunked DB writes. Investigate streaming/chunked import, lower peak memory, and add runtime diagnostics around daily_price_bundle_service.sync_from_github/import_daily_price_bundle.
close_reason: Implemented streaming daily price bundle import with bounded DB chunks and tests.

title: Fix issue #260 price refresh scan readiness mismatch
description: Implement the revised plan for GitHub issue #260: broad scan universes may proceed only when a completed refresh leaves a tiny stale tail (at least 99% fresh and no more than 100 omitted symbols). Omitted symbols must be excluded before scan dispatch/hash/feature-run lookup and surfaced through durable scan warning metadata. Custom/test or explicit symbol scans remain 100% strict and stale blocking details must identify sample stale symbols.
close_reason: Implemented high-threshold stale-tail scan freshness policy with durable warning metadata, strict custom/test blocking, API/bootstrap/UI warning surfaces, and regression coverage.

title: WS1B: feature_store filter perf — index/promote details_json preset fields
description: stock_feature_daily stores stage, rs_rating, minervini_score, ma_alignment etc. in details_json (unindexed). Proven live: min_composite (indexed col) 106ms vs stage/minervini (JSON) >35s. Fix: promote hot preset fields to real indexed columns (migration+backfill+write path in feature run) OR add expression indexes on json_extract(details_json,'$.field') (SQLite supports; confirm engine). Keep details_json for display/SE fields.
notes: Chose Postgres expression indexes over column promotion (ponytail: one migration, no write-path/backfill change, reversible). Discovered the app enforces PostgreSQL (app/database.py rejects sqlite), so live=Postgres. Migration 20260617_0021 creates (run_id, (CAST(details_json->>'field' AS FLOAT))) indexes for hot preset fields. Verified on Postgres via docker exec POC with exact app query + exact DDL + 4KB blobs: Seq Scan 302ms -> Bitmap Index Scan on ix_sfd_run_rs_rating 1.5ms. No code change needed (query already emits matching CAST expr). Postgres-guarded so SQLite test harness no-ops; test_alembic_baseline/test_main_migrations pass. Live verification: alembic upgrade + EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the US scan.
close_reason: Postgres expression-index migration implemented + committed (8d5478f). Verified via real Postgres POC: Seq Scan 302ms -> Bitmap Index Scan 1.5ms on exact app query. Live verification (alembic upgrade + EXPLAIN) tracked under b2l.

title: WS1A: two-phase query + lean count for scan_results path (legacy table)
description: scan_result_repo._scan_results_query selects full ScanResult (details JSON + sparkline blobs) + two unconditional outer joins; apply_sort_and_paginate runs query.count() over that heavy subquery -> 25-37s when filtered even though filter columns are indexed. Split into (1) lean selection/count query keyed by id, joins only when a joined column is filtered, (2) hydration query joining only the page's ~50 ids. Add unit test asserting count query emits no joins / no details+sparkline projection when no joined column filtered.
notes: Implemented lean count: apply_sort_and_paginate now uses _lean_count() (with_entities(func.count()) over same FROM/joins/WHERE) instead of Query.count()'s subquery-wrapped heavy SELECT, in both scan_result_query.py and feature_store_query.py. New test tests/unit/test_scan_result_lean_count.py (5 cases) asserts lean count == old .count() across filter shapes + flat SQL (no subquery, no details/sparkline projection); all pass. The page fetch is already LIMIT 50 so count() was the only unbounded op = the bottleneck. Conditional-joins/two-phase hydration deferred pending live confirmation that lean count alone suffices for scan_results. NOTE: 2 pre-existing unrelated test failures on origin/main (test_se_field_count expects 27 SE fields but map has 28 incl se_rs_line_blue_dot; test_unbound_scan_raises) — not caused by this change.
close_reason: Lean count implemented + committed (b7f6c4d), 5 tests pass. The 'two-phase/conditional-join' part of the title was deliberately deferred as a contingency — the page fetch is already LIMIT 50, so count() was the only unbounded op; reopen only if live shows lean count insufficient for scan_results. Live verification tracked under b2l.

title: Live preset filtering broken: filtered scan-results queries time out (30s axios) and show stale rows
description: Empirically confirmed on live site. Selecting a preset builds the correct filtered request but it exceeds the 30s axios timeout and aborts (net::ERR_ABORTED), leaving stale unfiltered rows labelled '(filtered)'. Two backend paths, two causes: scan_results (HK/JP) count()-over-heavy-double-join-subquery; stock_feature_daily (US) json_extract over unindexed details_json. Measured: HK 83ms->25.6s, JP 120ms->37.3s, US indexed-col filter 106ms vs JSON-field filter >35s. Parent tracking issue; see .plans/scan-filter-query-perf.md.

title: WS4: frontend — surface results-query timeout instead of silent stale '(filtered)' rows
description: getScanResults (frontend/src/api/scans.js:92) does not forward React Query AbortSignal; ScanPageContainer (:373) keeps stale rows visible and labels them '(filtered)' on a failed/aborted request. Forward AbortSignal, show loading/error on timeout, consider higher results timeout. De-scoped from the backend fix but filed so the symptom can't silently return.

title: WS2: investigate live scan result relevance (full-universe default, passes_only=1 vs 21)
description: Live default view = full scanned universe (~9867) sorted by score; top-by-minervini is 9.97/stage4. passes_only=true -> 1 row (SENEA, genuinely strong) while scan summary says 21 passed. Investigate defaulting results to passes_only/score-floor and reconcile the two pass definitions. Analysis first; propose change separately.
notes: ANALYSIS (no code change yet — product decision). Live results view loads the FULL scanned universe (~9867 US rows) ranked by composite_score; it does not default to 'passes'. passes_only=true (rating in Strong Buy/Buy) -> 1 row (SENEA). Scan summary shows '21 passed' (screener passes_template) — a DIFFERENT definition than passes_only's rating gate, hence 21 vs 1 confusion. KEY INSIGHT: the user's 'live is more relevant' is partly because presets currently time out (WS1A/1B bug), so they only ever see the score-sorted unfiltered top, which IS relevant at the top while the long tail is noise. Once filtering works (WS1A/1B), preset results will be properly curated. RECOMMENDATION: (1) reconcile the two pass definitions so the count next to a scan matches the results view; (2) consider a default relevance floor for the results view (composite_score>=60/'Watch'+ or top-N) instead of dumping the whole universe — but verify post-WS1 whether this is still needed. Defer implementation pending user product decision.

title: Make full backend pytest runnable without a live server
description: Running /Users/admin/StockScreenClaude/backend/venv/bin/pytest from backend collects tests/integration/test_production_performance.py, which executes at import time and posts to http://localhost:8000/api/v1/scans. In a normal local/CI test run without a live backend, collection exits with ConnectionRefusedError/SystemExit before the rest of the suite can run. Move this performance smoke behind an explicit marker, guard import-time execution, or document/run it separately so the default backend pytest gate is reliable.
notes: 2026-07-13 reproduction: default pytest also imports tests/integration/test_cache_warming.py at collection and calls localhost:8000. tests/integration/test_scan_api.py is another explicit live-server harness and should be marked/excluded from the default gate together with the production performance script.
close_reason: Resolved by stockscreenclaude-w1l

title: Stabilize backend full unit suite
description: During live UI loading work on 2026-06-15, focused daily snapshot tests passed, but a broader backend/tests/unit run was interrupted after 5m41s with 1,403 passes and 8 unrelated failures: MCP market overview snapshot task ordering, cache refresh workflow expectations, and orphaned scan cleanup SQLite uniqueness setup. Reproduce with backend venv pytest backend/tests/unit, isolate current expected market/task fixtures, and make the suite reliable again.
notes: 2026-07-13 reproduction on codex/grouped-scan-filter-builder: after excluding three explicit live-server harnesses, the broad backend run reached 1,215 passes with 17 unrelated failures before being stopped. Failures remain in theme-pipeline 503 integration tests, missing yfinance simulator market profiles, bootstrap-plan/golden MCP fixtures, cache-refresh expectations, and orphaned-scan SQLite setup. All grouped scan filter expression, capability, adapter parity, query, and endpoint tests passed.

title: Stabilize frontend UI test timeouts under full-suite runs
description: During the sparkline rollback cleanup on codex/improve-live-ui-loading, repeated frontend full-suite runs showed timeout/state-wait failures that moved between heavy UI tests (ThemesPage, RRGChart, StaticScanPage, AssistantWatchlistDialog, App.static). Focused reruns of some files passed, pointing to suite-level timing pressure or async cleanup issues rather than the rollback itself. Follow up by isolating the slow/flaky tests, fixing missing act/wait cleanup, and making npm run test:run reliable without requiring timing luck.
notes: 2026-06-15 follow-up evidence from codex/improve-live-ui-loading: npm run test:run -- --testTimeout=60000 produced 447/448 passing tests, with only src/components/AssistantChat/AssistantWatchlistDialog.test.jsx failing in the full run. Immediate isolated rerun via npm run test:run -- AssistantWatchlistDialog.test.jsx passed 3/3 in 6.12s. This reinforces suite-level async/timing isolation rather than an App.jsx regression.

title: Add runner guardrails to watchlists
description: Add objective runner-management fields to watchlist rows, including distance to key moving averages, extension from pivot, recent guardrail breaks, and optional stop/notes workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Watchlist rows display guardrail status for supported symbols; stock detail/watchlist chart can show guardrail context; users can preserve notes or stop rationale; tests cover computed fields and UI rendering.
